title: Golden Eyes
content:
Oh Amber Eyes, oh Golden Eyes!
        Oh Eyes so softly gay!
    Wherein swift fancies fall and rise,
        Grow dark and fade away.
    Ey

title: To The Unattainable
content:
Oh, that my blood were water, thou athirst,
    And thou and I in some far Desert land,
    How would I shed it gladly, if but first
    It tou

title: Verses: Faiz Ulla
content:
Just in the hush before dawn
    A little wistful wind is born.
    A little chilly errant breeze,
    That thrills the grasses, stirs the tree

title: The Masters
content:
Oh, Masters, you who rule the world,
    Will you not wait with me awhile,
    When swords are sheathed and sails are furled,
    And all the f

title: The Teak Forest
content:
Whether I loved you who shall say?
    Whether I drifted down your way
    In the endless River of Chance and Change,
    And you woke the stra

title: Back to the Border
content:
The tremulous morning is breaking
        Against the white waste of the sky,
    And hundreds of birds are awaking
        In tamarisk bushes

title: On the City Wall
content:
Upon the City Ramparts, lit up by sunset gleam,
    The Blue eyes that conquer, meet the Darker eyes that dream.

    The Dark eyes, so Eastern,

title: In the Early, Pearly Morning: Song by Valgovind
content:
The fields are full of Poppies, and the skies are very blue,
    By the Temple in the coppice, I wait, Beloved, for you.
    The level land is s

title: Reverie of Ormuz the Persian
content:
Softly the feathery Palm-trees fade in the violet Distance,
    Faintly the lingering light touches the edge of the sea,
    Sadly the Music of

title: My Desire
content:
Fate has given me many a gift
    To which men most aspire,
    Lovely, precious and costly things,
    But not my heart's desire.

    Many a

title: Rutland Gate
content:
His back is bent and his lips are blue,
    Shivering out in the wet:
    "Here's a florin, my man, for you,
    Go and get drunk and forget!"

title: The Lament of Yasmini, the Dancing-Girl
content:
Ah, what hast thou done with that Lover of mine?
    The Lover who only cared for thee?
    Mine for a handful of nights, and thine
    For the

title: The Cactus Thicket
content:
"The Atlas summits were veiled in purple gloom,
    But a golden moon above rose clear and free.
    The cactus thicket was ruddy with scarlet b

title: Khristna and His Flute
content:
(Translation by Moolchand)


    Be still, my heart, and listen,
    For sweet and yet acute
    I hear the wistful music
    Of Khristna and his

title: Atavism
content:
Deep in the jungle vast and dim,
    That knew not a white man's feet,
    I smelt the odour of sun-warmed fur,
    Musky, savage, and sweet.

title: The Aloe
content:
My life was like an Aloe flower, beneath an orient sky,
    Your sunshine touched it for an hour; it blossomed but to die.

    Torn up, cast ou

title: Song of Faiz Ulla
content:
Just at the time when Jasmins bloom, most sweetly in the summer weather,
    Lost in the scented Jungle gloom, one sultry night we spent together

title: Middle-age
content:
The sins of Youth are hardly sins,
    So frank they are and free.
    'T is but when Middle-age begins
    We need morality.

    Ah, pause a

title: Verse by Taj Mahomed
content:
When first I loved, I gave my very soul
    Utterly unreserved to Love's control,
    But Love deceived me, wrenched my youth away
    And made

title: The Rice-boat
content:
I slept upon the Rice-boat
    That, reef protected, lay
    At anchor, where the palm-trees
    Infringe upon the bay.
    The windless air w

title: The Net of Memory
content:
I cast the Net of Memory,
    Man's torment and delight,
    Over the level Sands of Youth
    That lay serenely bright,
    Their tranquil go

title: The Hut
content:
Dear little Hut by the rice-fields circled,
    That cocoa-nuts shade above.
    I hear the voices of children singing,
    And that means love

title: Lost Delight
content:
After the Hazara War

    I lie alone beneath the Almond blossoms,
        Where we two lay together in the spring,
    And now, as then, the mount

title: Malay Song
content:
The Stars await, serene and white,
        The unarisen moon;
    Oh, come and stay with me to-night,
        Beside the salt Lagoon!

    My

title: Lines by Taj Mahomed
content:
This passion is but an ember
        Of a Sun, of a Fire, long set;
    I could not live and remember,
        And so I love and forget.

title: Fate Knows no Tears
content:
Just as the dawn of Love was breaking
        Across the weary world of grey,
    Just as my life once more was waking
        As roses waken l

title: To the Hills!
content:
'T is eight miles out and eight miles in,
        Just at the break of morn.
    'T is ice without and flame within,
        To gain a kiss at

title: Nay, not To-night
content:
Nay, not to-night; - the slow, sad rain is falling
    Sorrowful tears, beneath a grieving sky,
    Far off a famished jackal, faintly calling,

title: Khan Zada's Song on the Hillside
content:
The fires that burn on all the hills
        Light up the landscape grey,
    The arid desert land distills
        The fervours of the day.

title: Song Of Khan Zada
content:
As one may sip a Stranger's Bowl
    You gave yourself but not your soul.
    I wonder, now that time has passed,
    Where you will come to re

title: The Bride
content:
Beat on the Tom-toms, and scatter the flowers,
    Jasmin, Hibiscus, vermillion and white,
    This is the day, and the Hour of Hours,
    Brin

title: Protest: By Zahir-u-Din
content:
Alas! alas! this wasted Night
    With all its Jasmin-scented air,
    Its thousand stars, serenely bright!
    I lie alone, and long for you,

title: Deserted Gipsy's Song: Hillside Camp
content:
She is glad to receive your turquoise ring,
         Dear and dark-eyed Lover of mine!
    I, to have given you everything:
         Beauty mad

title: Kashmiri Song
content:
Pale hands I love beside the Shalimar,
        Where are you now?    Who lies beneath your spell?
    Whom do you lead on Rapture's roadway, far

title: Marriage Thoughts: by Morsellin Khan
The First Wife
content:
Ah, my lord, are the tidings true,
    That thy mother's jewels are shapen anew?

    I hear that a bride has chosen been,
    The stars consul

title: The Jungle Flower
content:
Ah, the cool silence of the shaded hours,
    The scent and colour of the jungle flowers!

    Thou art one of the jungle flowers, strange and f

title: Adoration
content:
Who does not feel desire unending
        To solace through his daily strife,
    With some mysterious Mental Blending,
        The hungry lone

title: Song of Ramesram Temple Girl
content:
Now is the season of my youth,
    Not thus shall I always be,
    Listen, dear Lord, thou too art young,
    Take thy pleasure with me.
    M

title: Among the Rice Fields
content:
She was fair as a Passion-flower,
    (But little of love he knew.)
    Her lucent eyes were like amber wine,
    And her eyelids stained with

title: Two Songs by Sitara, of Kashmir
content:
Beloved! your hair was golden
    As tender tints of sunrise,
    As corn beside the River
         In softly varying hues.
    I loved you fo

title: Prayer
content:
You are all that is lovely and light,
        Aziza whom I adore,
    And, waking, after the night,
        I am weary with dreams of you.

title: Though in my Firmament thou wilt not shine
content:
Talk not, my Lord, of unrequited love,
    Since love requites itself most royally.
    Do we not live but by the sun above,
    And takes he a

title: Third Song, written during Fever (Three Songs of Zahir-u-Din)
content:
To-night the clouds hang very low,
        They take the Hill-tops to their breast,
        And lay their arms about the fields.
    The wind t

title: Sher Afzul
content:
This was the tale Sher Afzul told to me,
    While the spent camels bubbled on their knees,
    And ruddy camp-fires twinkled through the gloom

title: Sampan Song
content:
A little breeze blew over the sea,
        And it came from far away,
    Across the fields of millet and rice,
    All warm with sunshine and

title: The First Lover
content:
As o'er the vessel's side she leant,
        She saw the swimmer in the sea
    With eager eyes on her intent,
        "Come down, come down an

title: Mahomed Akram's Appeal To The Stars
content:
Oh, Silver Stars that shine on what I love,
        Touch the soft hair and sparkle in the eyes, - 
    Send, from your calm serenity above,

title: Feroke
content:
The rice-birds fly so white, so silver white,
    The velvet rice-flats lie so emerald green,
    My heart inhales, with sorrowful delight,

title: Wings
content:
Was it worth while to forego our wings
    To gain these dextrous hands ?
    Truly they fashion us wonderful things
    As the fancy of man de

title: Reverie: Zahir-u-Din
content:
Alone, I wait, till her twilight gate
        The Night slips quietly through,
    With shadow and gloom, and purple bloom,
        Flung over

title: The Window Overlooking the Harbour
content:
Sad is the Evening: all the level sand
        Lies left and lonely, while the restless sea,
    Tired of the green caresses of the land,

title: Reminiscence of Mahomed Akram
content:
I shall never forget you, never.    Never escape
    Your memory woven about the beautiful things of life.

    The sudden Thought of your Face

title: Fancy
content:
Far in the Further East the skilful craftsman
        Fashioned this fancy for the West's delight.
    This rose and azure Dragon, crouching sof

title: The Garden by the Bridge
content:
The Desert sands are heated, parched and dreary,
        The tigers rend alive their quivering prey
    In the near Jungle; here the kites rise,

title: Unforgotten
content:
Do you ever think of me? you who died
        Ere our Youth's first fervour chilled,
    With your soft eyes and your pulses stilled
        Ly

title: Song of Jasoda
I Shall Forget
content:
Although my life, which thou hast scarred and shaken,
    Retains awhile some influence of thee,
    As shells, by faithless waves long since fo

title: Till I Wake
content:
When I am dying, lean over me tenderly, softly,
        Stoop, as the yellow roses droop in the wind from the South.
    So I may, when I wake,

title: Youth
content:
I am not sure if I knew the truth
        What his case or crime might be,
    I only know that he pleaded Youth,
        A beautiful, golden p

title: The Garden of Kama: Kama the Indian Eros
content:
The daylight is dying,
    The Flying fox flying,
         Amber and amethyst burn in the sky.
    See, the sun throws a late,
    Lingering,

title: Second Song: The Girl from Baltistan
content:
Throb, throb, throb,
    Far away in the blue transparent Night,
    On the outer horizon of a dreaming consciousness,
    She hears the s

title: Verses
content:
You are my God, and I would fain adore You
        With sweet and secret rites of other days.
    Burn scented oil in silver lamps before You,

title: There is no Breeze to Cool the Heat of Love
content:
The listless Palm-trees catch the breeze above
        The pile-built huts that edge the salt Lagoon,
    There is no Breeze to cool the heat of

title: Farewell
content:
Farewell, Aziz, it was not mine to fold you
        Against my heart for any length of days.
    I had no loveliness, alas, to hold you,

title: Song of the Parao (Camping-ground)
content:
Heart, my heart, thou hast found thy home!
    From gloom and sorrow thou hast come forth,
    Thou who wast foolish, and sought to roam
    'N

title: The Convert
content:
The sun was hot on the tamarind trees,
    Their shadows shrivelled and shrank.
    No coolness came on the off-shore breeze
    That rattled t

title: Song of the Peri
content:
Beauty, the Gift of Gifts, I give to thee.
    Pleasure and love shall spring around thy feet
    As through the lake the lotuses arise
    Pin

title: Shivratri (the Night of Shiva)
content:
(While the procession passed at Ramesram)


    Nearer and nearer cometh the car
    Where the Golden Goddess towers,
    Sweeter and sweeter grows

title: Oh, Unforgotten and Only Lover
content:
Oh, unforgotten and only lover,
    Many years have swept us apart,
    But none of the long dividing seasons
    Slay your memory in my heart.

title: Love Lightly
content:
There were Roses in the hedges, and Sunshine in the sky,
    Red Lilies in the sedges, where the water rippled by,
    A thousand Bulbuls singin

title: Song of Taj Mahomed
content:
Dear is my inlaid sword; across the Border
    It brought me much reward; dear is my Mistress,
    The jewelled treasure of an amorous hour.

title: Camp Follower's Song, Gomal River
content:
We have left Gul Kach behind us,
         Are marching on Apozai, - 
    Where pleasure and rest are waiting
         To welcome us by and by.

title: Story by Lalla-ji, the Priest
content:
He loved the Plant with a keen delight,
        A passionate fervour, strange to see,
    Tended it ardently, day and night,
        Yet never

title: Written in Cananore
content:
I

    Who was it held that Love was soothing or sweet?
    Mine is a painful fire, at its whitest heat.

    Who said that Beauty was ever a g

title: Story of Udaipore: Told by Lalla-ji, the Priest
content:
"And when the Summer Heat is great,
        And every hour intense,
         The Moghra, with its subtle flowers,
        Intoxicates the

title: Valgovind's Boat Song
content:
Waters glisten and sunbeams quiver,
        The wind blows fresh and free.
    Take my boat to your breast, O River!
        Carry me out to Se

title: Ashore
content:
Out I came from the dancing-place:
    The night-wind met me face to face - 

    A wind off the harbour, cold and keen,
    "I know," it whist

title: His Rubies: Told by Valgovind
content:
Along the hot and endless road,
        Calm and erect, with haggard eyes,
    The prisoner bore his fetters' load
        Beneath the scorchin

title: The Regret of the Ranee in the Hall of Peacocks
content:
This man has taken my Husband's life
        And laid my Brethren low,
    No sister indeed, were I, no wife,
        To pardon and let him go.

title: No Rival Like the Past
content:
As those who eat a Luscious Fruit, sunbaked,
        Full of sweet juice, with zest, until they find
    It finished, and their appetite unslake

title: When Love is Over - Song of Khan Zada
content:
Only in August my heart was aflame,
        Catching the scent of your Wind-stirred hair,
    Now, though you spread it to soften my sleep

title: Disappointment
content:
Oh, come, Beloved, before my beauty fades,
    Pity the sorrow of my loneliness.
    I am a Rosebush that the Cypress shades,
    No sunbeams f

title: Kotri, by the River
content:
At Kotri, by the river, when the evening's sun is low,
    The waving palm trees quiver, the golden waters glow,
    The shining ripples shiver,

title: This Month the Almonds Bloom at Kandahar
content:
I hate this City, seated on the Plain,
        The clang and clamour of the hot Bazar,
    Knowing, amid the pauses of my pain,
        This mo

title: The Tom-toms
content:
Dost thou hear the tom-toms throbbing,
    Like a lonely lover sobbing
    For the beauty that is robbing him of all his life's delight?
    Pl

title: Famine Song
content:
Death and Famine on every side
        And never a sign of rain,
    The bones of those who have starved and died
        Unburied upon the pla

title: Reverie Of Mahomed Akram At The Tamarind Tank
content:
The Desert is parched in the burning sun
    And the grass is scorched and white.
    But the sand is passed, and the march is done,
    We are

title: The Rao of Ilore
content:
I was sold to the Rao of Ilore,
    Slender and tall was he.
    When his litter carried him down the street
    I peeped through the thatch to

title: Hira-Singh's Farewell to Burmah
content:
On the wooden deck of the wooden Junk, silent, alone, we lie,
    With silver foam about the bow, and a silver moon in the sky:
    A glimmer of

title: To M. C. N.
content:
Thou hast no wealth, nor any pride of power,
    Thy life is offered on affection's altar.
    Small sacrifices claim thee, hour by hour,
    Y

title: My Paramour was Loneliness
content:
My paramour was loneliness
    And lying by the sea,
    Soft songs of sorrow and distress
    He did beget in me.

    Later another lover ca

title: Zira: In Captivity
content:
Love me a little, Lord, or let me go,
    I am so weary walking to and fro
    Through all your lonely halls that were so sweet
    Did they bu

title: Yasin Khan
content:
Ay, thou has found thy kingdom, Yasin Khan,
    Thy fathers' pomp and power are thine, at last.
    No more the rugged roads of Khorasan,
    T

title: Ojira, to Her Lover
content:
I am waiting in the desert, looking out towards the sunset,
    And counting every moment till we meet.
    I am waiting by the marshes and I tr

title: To The Unattainable: Lament Of Mahomed Akram
content:
I would have taken Golden Stars from the sky for your necklace,
    I would have shaken rose-leaves for your rest from all the rose-trees.

title: Vayu the Wind
content:
Ah, Wind, I have always loved thee
    Since those far off nights
    When I lay beneath the vines
    A prey to strange delights,
    For amo

title: Second Song (Three Songs of Zahir-u-Din)
content:
How much I loved that way you had
    Of smiling most, when very sad,
    A smile which carried tender hints
        Of delicate tints

title: Early Love
content:
Who says I wrong thee, my half-opened rose?
    Little he knows of thee or me, or love. - 
    I am so tender of thy fragile youth,
    Yea, in

title: Lalila, to the Ferengi Lover
content:
Why above others was I so blessed
         And honoured? to be chosen one
    To hold you, sleeping, against my breast,
         As now I may h

title: Song of the Colours: by Taj Mahomed
content:
Rose-colour
    Rose Pink am I, the colour gleams and glows
        In many a flower; her lips, those tender doors
    By which, in time of lov

title: Kashmiri Song by Juma
content:
You never loved me, and yet to save me,
    One unforgetable night you gave me
    Such chill embraces as the snow-covered heights
    Receive

title: Listen, Beloved
content:
Listen, Beloved, the Casurinas quiver,
    Each tassel prays the wind to set it free,
    Hark to the frantic sobbing of the river,
    Wild to

title: Palm Trees by the Sea
content:
Love, let me thank you for this!
        Now we have drifted apart,
    Wandered away from the sea, - 
        For the fresh touch of your kiss

title: The Rice was under Water
content:
The Rice was under water, and the land was scourged with rain,
    The nights were desolation, and the day was born in pain.
    Ah, the famine

title: Valgovind's Song in the Spring
content:
The Temple bells are ringing,
    The young green corn is springing,
         And the marriage month is drawing very near.

    I lie hidden in

title: Sea Song
content:
Against the planks of the cabin side,
        (So slight a thing between them and me,)
    The great waves thundered and throbbed and sighed,

title: Yasmini
content:
At night, when Passion's ebbing tide
        Left bare the Sands of Truth,
    Yasmini, resting by my side,
        Spoke softly of her youth.

title: Surface Rights
content:
Drifting, drifting down the River,
    Tawny current and foam-flecked tide,
    Sorrowful songs of lonely boatmen,
    Mournful forests on eith

title: Feroza
content:
The evening sky was as green as Jade,
         As Emerald turf by Lotus lake,
    Behind the Kafila far she strayed,
         (The Pearls are l

title: Thoughts: Mahomed Akram
content:
If some day this body of mine were burned
    (It found no favour alas! with you)
    And the ashes scattered abroad, unurned,
    Would Love d

title: Starlight
content:
O beautiful Stars, when you see me go
        Hither and thither, in search of love,
    Do you think me faithless, who gleam and glow

title: Dedication to Malcolm Nicolson
content:
I, who of lighter love wrote many a verse,
    Made public never words inspired by thee,
    Lest strangers' lips should carelessly rehearse

title: The Plains
content:
How one loves them
    These wide horizons; whether Desert or Sea, - 
        Vague and vast and infinite; faintly clear - 
    Surely, hi

title: I Arise and go Down to the River
content:
I arise and go down to the River, and currents that come from the sea,
    Still fresh with the salt of the ocean, are lovely and precious to me,

title: Unanswered
content:
Something compels me, somewhere.    Yet I see
    No clear command in Life's long mystery.

    Oft have I flung myself beside my horse,
    To

title: Story of Lilavanti
content:
They lay the slender body down
        With all its wealth of wetted hair,
    Only a daughter of the town,
        But very young and slight a

title: The Temple Dancing Girl
content:
You will be mine; those lightly dancing feet,
        Falling as softly on the careless street
    As the wind-loosened petals of a flower,

title: Lallji my Desire
content:
"This is no time for saying 'no'"
    Were thy last words to me,
    And yet my lips refused the kiss
    They might have given thee.

title: On Pilgrimage
content:
Oh, youthful bearer of my palanquin,
    Thy glossy hair lies loosened on thy neck,
    The "tears of labour" gem thy velvet skin,
    Whose ev

title: Less Than The Dust
content:
Less than the dust, beneath thy Chariot wheel,
    Less than the rust, that never stained thy Sword,
    Less than the trust thou hast in me, O

title: The Dying Prince
content:
There are no days for me any more, for the dawn is dark with tears,
    There is no rest for me any more, for the night is thick with fears.

title: The Singer
content:
The singer only sang the Joy of Life,
        For all too well, alas! the singer knew
    How hard the daily toil, how keen the strife,

title: Three Songs of Zahir-u-Din
content:
The tropic day's redundant charms
        Cool twilight soothes away,
    The sun slips down behind the palms
        And leaves the landscape

title: Afridi Love
content:
Since, Oh, Beloved, you are not even faithful
        To me, who loved you so, for one short night,
    For one brief space of darkness, though

title: Request
content:
Give me your self one hour; I do not crave
        For any love, or even thought, of me.
    Come, as a Sultan may caress a slave
        And t

title: From Behind the Lattice
content:
I see your red-gold hair and know
    How white the hidden skin must be,
    Though sun-kissed face and fingers show
    The fervour of the noo

title: Song of the Devoted Slave
content:
There is one God: Mahomed his Prophet.    Had I his power
    I would take the topmost peaks of the snow-clad Himalayas,
    And would range the

title: Memory
content:
How I loved you in your sleep,
    With the starlight on your hair!

    The touch of your lips was sweet,
        Aziza whom I adore,
    I l

title: Song by Gulbaz
content:
"Is it safe to lie so lonely when the summer twilight closes
    No companion maidens, only you asleep among the roses?

    "Thirteen, fourteen

title: Malaria
content:
He lurks among the reeds, beside the marsh,
        Red oleanders twisted in His hair,
    His eyes are haggard and His lips are harsh,

title: Sunstroke
content:
Oh, straight, white road that runs to meet,
         Across green fields, the blue green sea,
    You knew the little weary feet
         Of my
